parsed_review.summary: CP00-738 (RP24.P05.M05.S13-S22, risk A, 10 units, micro "Permission And Audit Binding") is a clean, descriptor-only continuation of the CP722-CP737 Korean Legal Depth pattern, and its production_ready claim — the descriptor-verified flag korean_legal_p05_permission_audit_evidence_bridge_descriptor_verified, NOT a runtime/enterprise-trust claim — is supported. Verified by direct source inspection: (1) Implementation wiring is consistent across registry.js (KOREAN_LEGAL_CP738_PACK_BINDING L281, REQUIREMENTS L2652), service.js (case set/descriptor/hermes/claude/handoff L86-87,141-143), validators.js (coverage+descriptor L58-60), index.js (export *), README CP00-738, and the regenerated contract projection (latest_pack/latest_projection = CP00-738). (2) Deliverable distribution matches the prompt exactly (implementation 5, fixture 1, test 3, hermes_evidence 1 = 10). (3) Tests model.test.js L41-43 cover binding/coverage, runtime-closed authority boundary, and the contract projection (validation.valid=true, cp722-cp737 historical, cp738 coverage+descriptor errors=[]). (4) Boundaries hold everywhere: NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION (descriptor_only, no_real_data, synthetic_only, writes_product_state=false) applied via freezeResult; descriptor rows enforce descriptor_only/runtime_execution=false and exclude raw/legal-source/credential/client payloads; HWPX/court-filing/litigation/corporate/clause runtime all closed. (5) Hermes pattern is used only for deterministic descriptor evidence packets (emits_runtime_receipt=false, receipt_shape descriptor_only_korean_legal_no_write), not product runtime; hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false. (6) Claude stays read-only and non-final (claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true); manifest/command-evidence/contract show no production_ready=true, no runtime opened, no enterprise-trust claim. (7) Manifest honestly reports production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, status in_progress; changed-file-scope correctly isolates pack scope from unrelated dirty UI/matter/ldip files. Readiness auth-preflight passed (exit 0, 1447-byte stdout). The pending state of adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json, and claude-review-result.json is expected — they await this receipt and are not a defect. No P0/P1/P2 issues. Two non-blocking P3 transparency notes follow. Note: validation command PASS/FAIL outcomes are taken from command-evidence.json (this is a read-only, no-Bash review); I corroborated them through source and artifact cross-checks, not re-execution.

findings.title: Validation outcomes are evidence-reported, not re-executed in this read-only review
findings.details: command-evidence.json records all required gates (closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check) plus build and node --test as exit_code 0. As a read-only reviewer without Bash, I did not re-execute these; I corroborated them by direct source inspection (registry/service/validators/index/test wiring) and artifact cross-checks (contract validation block cp738 coverage+descriptor errors=[], validation.valid=true). This is consistent with the defined read-tools/summary-only review mode and is not a defect.
findings.recommendation: Final human/orchestrator approval retains authority to confirm CI re-execution before any future enterprise-trust or runtime claim; no action needed for this descriptor-only CP closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: informational; consistent with defined read-only review mode; non-blocking

findings.title: Live plan/queue control-plane files are dirty and classified unrelated though they reflect CP00-738 generation
findings.details: docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json and next-pack-queue.json appear under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, yet they reflect CP00-738's own regeneration (plan marks CP00-738 live/pack_number 738; queue live_latest_pack_id=CP00-738, preview CP00-739; generated_at 2026-06-15T09:18:08 matches the closeout-pack-plan:generate step in command-evidence). The pack remains self-contained because manifest.plan_binding_snapshot embeds the CP00-738 plan binding, so coverage validation does not depend on the dirty plan file. This is the established control-plane separation, not a scope error.
findings.recommendation: Confirm the closeout (or paired control-plane) commit captures the intended plan/queue state so the live cursor advancing to CP00-739/RP24.P05.M06.S01 is persisted as expected.
